message:
feat(core): harden input validation, failure-atomic timer update, and overflow-safe metrics

Add defensive NULL/range checks across six modules and make
timer_update failure-atomic by registering the replacement before
retiring the old timer.  Extract metrics_clamp_add_i64 helper to
deduplicate overflow-safe addition in counter increment and histogram
observe paths.  profile_compat now fails closed for out-of-range
property/rule queries.

Modules touched:
- adaptive: reject out-of-range fallback index and NULL evidence with
  nonzero count.
- circuit_breaker: reject zero half_open_max_calls at init.
- observability: factor clamped-add into helper; apply to counter and
  histogram.
- profile_compat: bounds-check property class and semantic rule
  lookups, fail closed to ASX_PROP_SEMANTIC / not-enforced.
- stream: add NULL guards to fold, count, and for_each terminals.
- timer_wheel: reorder update to register-then-cancel (failure-atomic);
  update doc comments to reflect new semantics.

Tests: 12 new test cases covering every new guard and the
failure-atomic timer update (resource-exhausted and duration-exceeded
paths).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Ensure links to downstream phase/gate beads are consistent.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Open decisions are resolved and reflected in the published decision log.
- Prevents accidental scope expansion during implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:11.937528181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:27:11.740784831Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:27:11.740759724Z","close_reason":"Consolidated deferred-surface register + owner decision log, added decision crosswalk and downstream bead/gate alignment, and codified pending-decision queue to eliminate implicit scope drift","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-0","planning","scope"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-296.7","depends_on_id":"bd-296","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-296.7","depends_on_id":"bd-296.24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-296.7","depends_on_id":"bd-296.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-296.8","title":"Create plan-to-execution traceability index (claims -> beads -> tests -> artifacts)","description":"Background:
Future maintainers should not need to re-read the giant plan to understand why work exists.

Subtasks:
- Build section-by-section index mapping plan sections 0 through 21 to bead IDs, acceptance artifacts, and owning milestones.
- Include mapping from quality gates to concrete CI jobs/artifacts and failure triage commands.
- Include mapping from profile promises to fixture families, digest checks, and metric budgets.
- Include mapping for open decisions, deferred surfaces, and risk controls so governance context is self-contained.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every major plan section has at least one mapped bead and acceptance artifact.
- Index is self-contained and update protocol is documented.
- Enables audit/review without archaeology and without reopening the plan  {"id":"bd-2cw","title":"EPIC: Runtime Kernel, Scheduling, Cancellation, Timer, and Channel Semantics","description":"Background:
Kernel behavior is where semantic fidelity is most fragile: ordering, cancellation, and queueing rules can silently diverge.

Goal:
Implement deterministic kernel loop and channel/timer semantics with rigorous fairness/starvation and correctness checks.

Must include:
- deterministic tie-break and event sequencing
- cancellation propagation checkpoints
- timer wheel semantics and O(1) cancel behavior
- bounded two-phase MPSC semantics

Why this serves users:
- reproducible behavior for incident analysis
- robust performance and correctness on both low-latency and constrained hardware.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.106645851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T23:12:07.786224004Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T23:12:07.786197254Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-5","phase-6","runtime","scheduler"]}
 {"id":"bd-2cw.1","title":"Implement arena-backed region/task/obligation tables with generation-safe handles","description":"Background:
Runtime storage for lifecycle entities must be performant and safe under churn, cancellation, and reuse.

Subtasks:
- Implement arena-backed table structures for regions, tasks, and obligations.
- Enforce generation-safe handle validation on every access path.
- Integrate debug quarantine behavior for hardening mode.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Table operations are deterministic and test-covered.
- Handle reuse and stale-handle cases are explicitly validated.
- No direct external mutable references escape API boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.177963236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:14:44.551376753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:14:44.551358800Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-4","runtime","tables"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cw.2","title":"Implement deterministic scheduler loop with stable tie-break ordering and event sequencing","description":"Background:
Scheduler ordering drift is a primary semantic regression vector and must be tightly controlled.

Subtasks:
- Implement deterministic ready/cancel/timed lane processing rules.
- Define stable tie-break ordering for simultaneous events.
- Emit deterministic sequencing metadata suitable for replay identity.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Same input and seed produce identical ordered event streams.
- Ordering rules are documented and fixture-tested.
- No hidden nondeterminism from container iteration order.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.342327824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:41:00.394562998Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:41:00.394541057Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","phase-5","runtime","scheduler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-aw69","title":"EPIC: Embedded Target Matrix Gate (GATE-EMBEDDED)","description":"## Goal
Implement GATE-EMBEDDED: cross-compilation for ARM and RISC-V targets with QEMU-based smoke
tests, validating that the C port builds and runs correctly on constrained embedded platforms.

## Why This Matters
The C port's raison d'être is portability to embedded targets that Rust's async runtime cannot
reach (bare-metal MCUs, router-class devices, automotive ECUs). Without actually building and
testing on these targets, the "portable ANSI C" claim is aspirational rather than proven.

GATE-EMBEDDED is one of the 9 non-negotiable quality gates from AGENTS.md:
"Embedded target matrix gate (cross-build + QEMU + device smoke)."

The three resource classes (R1=tight, R2=balanced, R3=roomy) defined in asx_config.h are
specifically designed for embedded deployment. The FREESTANDING and EMBEDDED_ROUTER profiles
compile with no libc dependency. This gate proves those profiles work end-to-end.

## What "Done" Looks Like
- Cross-compilation succeeds for: arm-none-eabi (Cortex-M), aarch64-linux-gnu, riscv32-unknown-elf
- QEMU user-mode runs the C test suite for aarch64 and riscv32 targets
- Bare-metal builds (arm-none-eabi) link successfully with FREESTANDING profile
- Resource class R1 builds fit within 64KB RAM budget
- make embedded-matrix passes in CI

## Architectural Context
The Makefile already has profile-parity targets that build with ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING and
ASX_PROFILE_EMBEDDED_ROUTER. The freestanding hooks (src/platform/freestanding/hooks.c) provide
stub implementations for platforms without libc. What's missing is the actual cross-compiler
toolchains, QEMU setup, and the CI integration to exercise them.

## Risk
- Cross-compiler toolchain installation varies by host OS
- QEMU user-mode may not perfectly emulate all target quirks
- Bare-metal linking requires a linker script and startup code
- R1 memory budget (64KB) may require tuning ASX_MAX_* constants","notes":"## CRITICAL: EXISTING INFRASTRUCTURE AUDIT (2026-03-12)

The following ALREADY EXISTS and must NOT be recreated or modified:
- tools/ci/run_embedded_matrix.sh: Linux-musl cross-build matrix (armv7, aarch64, mipsel)
- tools/ci/run_qemu_smoke.sh: QEMU testing for Linux-musl targets
- make ci-embedded-matrix: Makefile target for Linux-musl embedded builds
- tools/ci/generate_layout_budget_report.sh: Binary size/layout reporting
- tools/ci/compute_layout_budget_delta.sh: Cross-run regression detection

## What This Epic ACTUALLY Adds (COMPLEMENTARY, NOT REPLACEMENT)
Bare-metal cross-compilation for FREESTANDING/EMBEDDED_ROUTER profiles on:
- ARM Cortex-M (arm-none-eabi-gcc) — new toolchain
- RISC-V (riscv-elf-gcc) — new toolchain
- QEMU semihosting for bare-metal binaries — new execution mode
- R1 memory budgets for .text/.bss sections — new measurements

## Subtask Inventory (8 total)
.1 Install bare-metal ARM toolchain (60m) — Linux-musl ARM already exists
.2 Install RISC-V toolchain (60m) — genuinely new
.3 QEMU semihosting setup (90m) — Linux user-mode QEMU already exists
.4 Bare-metal Makefile targets (120m) — Linux-musl targets already exist
.5 Bare-metal link validation (120m) — genuinely new
.6 R1 memory budgets (90m) — layout reporting exists for musl
.7 CI gate for bare-metal (120m) — embedded CI exists for musl
.8 QEMU e2e tests for bare-metal (180m) — QEMU smoke exists for musl","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":840,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:06:25.215072286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:39:35.547567356Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:39:35.546118414Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embedded","phase3","portability"]}
 {"id":"bd-aw69.1","title":"Install ARM cross-compiler toolchain (arm-none-eabi-gcc)","description":"## STATUS: LINUX-MUSL TOOLCHAINS EXIST — BARE-METAL TOOLCHAIN IS NEW

## Context
The project already has cross-compilation for Linux-musl ARM targets:
- armv7-openwrt-linux-muslgnueabi-gcc (OpenWrt SDK)
- aarch64-openwrt-linux-musl-gcc (OpenWrt SDK)
These are LINUX embedded targets (musl libc, runs under Linux kernel).

This bead is about BARE-METAL ARM targets (arm-none-eabi-gcc) which are a
DIFFERENT, COMPLEMENTARY class of embedded deployment:
- No OS, no libc (or only newlib-nano)
- Targets Cortex-M0/M3/M4/M7 (microcontrollers, not application processors)
- Validates the FREESTANDING profile (no POSIX, no malloc, no printf)

## Why Both Are Needed
- Linux-musl embedded (existing): Proves the library works on resource-constrained
  Linux systems like routers (OpenWrt). Uses EMBEDDED_ROUTER profile.
- Bare-metal (this bead): Proves the library works on microcontrollers with NO OS.
  Uses FREESTANDING profile. This is a stronger portability claim.

## Task
1. Install arm-none-eabi-gcc (GNU ARM Embedded Toolchain)
2. Verify targets: cortex-m0, cortex-m3, cortex-m4, cortex-m7
3. Verify newlib-nano is available
4. Create smoke test compiling a trivial .c file
5. Document in tools/ci/TOOLCHAINS.md (alongside existing musl entries)

## Acceptance Criteria
- arm-none-eabi-gcc --version succeeds
- Can compile targeting -mcpu=cortex-m4 -mthumb
- newlib-nano headers findable
- Clearly documented as complementary to existing musl toolchains","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:12:10.808053668Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:52:07.767510854Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:52:07.765301129Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.1","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:12:10.808053668Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-aw69.2","title":"Install RISC-V cross-compiler toolchain (riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc)","description":"## Context
The GATE-EMBEDDED quality gate also requires RISC-V target validation. RISC-V is
increasingly important for embedded systems and the asupersync library should be
verified on this architecture.

## Background
RISC-V (specifically RV32IMAC and RV64IMAC) is a primary embedded target alongside
ARM. The C port's portability claims must be validated on both architectures to be
credible. Key concerns:
- Endianness handling (RISC-V is little-endian, same as ARM, but the codec must
  handle big-endian fields correctly regardless)
- Alignment requirements differ from ARM
- Atomic operation availability varies by extension set (A extension)

## Task
1. Install riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc (or riscv-none-elf-gcc from SiFive/xPack)
2. Verify targets: rv32imac, rv64imac
3. Verify that picolibc or newlib headers are available
4. Create a small smoke test compiling a trivial .c file
5. Document in tools/ci/TOOLCHAINS.md alongside ARM entry

## Acceptance Criteria
- `riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc --version` succeeds
- Can compile targeting -march=rv32imac -mabi=ilp32
- Can compile targeting -march=rv64imac -mabi=lp64

## Why This Matters
Validates portability claims on a non-ARM embedded architecture. Together with ARM,
provides confidence that the FREESTANDING profile is genuinely cross-platform.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:12:18.821067763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:53:01.452593964Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:53:01.447336167Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.2","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:12:18.821067763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -241,7 +243,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ekb3.4","title":"Audit record: constants and comments verified as correct (no change needed)","description":"## Purpose

This bead documents items that were examined during the audit and explicitly determined
to NOT need changes. This prevents future auditors from re-investigating the same items.

## Hardcoded Values Verified as Intentionally Correct

### 1. hooks.c:62 — default_wall_clock returns 0
- **Value**: `return 0;`
- **Why correct**: This is an intentional platform hook stub. The walking skeleton runtime
  provides default hooks that are replaced at runtime init. The wall_clock hook returns 0
  because deterministic mode doesn't need real time, and non-deterministic deployments
  MUST register a real clock hook via asx_runtime_hooks_init(). Returning 0 is fail-safe:
  it makes time-dependent logic behave deterministically rather than using garbage values.

### 2. lab.c:57,93 — tick_ns defaults to 1000000 (1ms)
- **Value**: `cfg->tick_ns = 1000000ULL;`
- **Why correct**: This IS a configurable default — it's set in the asx_lab_config struct
  which users fill before calling asx_lab_init(). The literal appears in config_init (setting
  the default) and in lab_init (fallback if user left it 0). Users override it by setting
  cfg.tick_ns before init. No #define needed because the config struct IS the override mechanism.

### 3. console.c:84 — "would check isatty()"
- **Value**: Comment explaining platform-specific behavior
- **Why correct**: The comment says "A real platform adapter would check isatty() and TERM
  environment variable." This is a design note explaining what the platform hook should do,
  not a promise of future work. The CORE profile doesn't have terminal awareness by design.

### 4. plan.c:413 — "this would set up a deadline"
- **Value**: Comment explaining async vs deterministic behavior
- **Why correct**: The comment says "In a real async runtime, this would set up a deadline."
  This explains that the deterministic walking skeleton doesn't use real deadlines, which
  is the intended behavior for CORE profile. Not stale — it's architecture documentation.

### 5. Profile overload thresholds in overload_catalog.c (90%, 85%, 80%, 75%)
- **Values**: Percentages like 90, 85, 80, 75
- **Why correct**: These are intentional per-profile policy constants, not magic numbers.
  Each profile defines specific overload thresholds as part of its operational envelope.
  The README explicitly says "Profiles control operational envelopes (limits, defaults,
  instrumentation), never semantic behavior." These ARE the per-profile defaults.

### 6. Seqlock EBR spike retry limits (100, 1000)
- **Values**: `max_retries = 100` and `max_spins = 1000`
- **Why correct**: The file is `seqlock_ebr_spike.c` — a research spike for experimental
  lock-free data structures. Spike code uses hardcoded values by design; if the spike
  graduates to production, the values should be extracted at that time. Premature
  abstraction of spike code adds overhead without benefit.

### 7. Config default timeouts in config_types.c (10s, 30s, 5s)
- **Values**: Various timeout defaults
- **Why correct**: These appear in config builder functions that produce default
  configurations. Users override them via the config struct before passing to init.
  The config struct IS the customization mechanism — no #define needed.

## Methodology

Each item was found via:
- `grep -rn 'TODO\|FIXME\|HACK\|XXX' src/` (zero results)
- `grep -rni ' will \| would ' src/*.c include/*.h`
- Manual inspection of all numeric literals in function bodies across runtime/, core/, net/

Items were classified as "needs fix" or "verified correct" based on:
1. Is the value user-facing or deployment-sensitive? → needs fix
2. Is there already a config mechanism? → verified correct
3. Is it a spike/research file? → verified correct (defer until graduation)
4. Is the "will/would" describing actual planned work or explaining semantics? → varies

 {"id":"bd-rkql.6","title":"Unit tests for fuzz input grammar parser and cross-language determinism","description":"## Context
The fuzz input grammar ALREADY EXISTS in tests/fuzz/fuzz_differential.c (21 op types,
xoshiro256** PRNG, FNV-1a digest). The Rust target (bd-rkql.3) must replicate it
byte-exactly. This bead verifies cross-language determinism.

## What Needs To Be Done
1. Extract grammar test vectors from fuzz_differential.c:
   - For seeds 0..99, generate scenarios using the C code
   - Capture: seed → list of (op_kind, idx_a, idx_b, arg_u32, arg_u64)
   - Save as tests/fuzz/grammar_test_vectors.json

2. Create a C test that verifies the vectors (regression test for the C grammar):
   - tests/unit/fuzz/test_grammar_determinism.c
   - For each vector: generate from seed, compare to expected op sequence
   - This catches accidental grammar changes in future C edits

3. Create a Rust test that verifies the SAME vectors:
   - Rust target parses each seed, generates scenario, compares to expected
   - 100% match with C required

4. Create cross-language determinism script (tests/fuzz/test_cross_determinism.sh):
   - Run C scenario generator for seeds 0..999
   - Run Rust scenario generator for seeds 0..999
   - Diff outputs — must be identical
   - JSONL logging per seed

## Acceptance Criteria
- 100+ test vectors extracted and saved
- C regression test passes
- Rust test produces identical op sequences for all vectors
- Cross-language determinism script passes for 1000 seeds
- JSONL log output for CI

## Why This Is Critical
Without this, differential fuzzing results are untrustworthy — a "divergence" could
be a grammar parser difference rather than a behavioral parity issue.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:27:09.756941258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:52:29.459435632Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T23:52:29.458283049Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.6","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:27:09.756941258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.6","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:27:14.935929873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.6","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:27:15.911073727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-rkql.7","title":"Integrate GATE-FUZZ into CI pipeline with iteration budget tiers","description":"## STATUS: PARTIALLY EXISTS — EXTEND FOR RUST COMPARISON

## Context
The Makefile ALREADY HAS fuzz targets:
- make fuzz-build: builds fuzz harnesses
- make fuzz-smoke: runs 100-iteration smoke test
- make fuzz-run: runs default 1000 iterations
- make fuzz-nightly: runs 100K iterations

These run C-only determinism self-checks. What's MISSING is:
- Rust binary comparison (differential C-vs-Rust)
- GATE-FUZZ as a named CI gate with structured JSONL output
- Iteration budget tiers (smoke/nightly/release) with Rust comparison

## Task
1. Extend existing Makefile fuzz targets to support RUST_FUZZ_BINARY variable:
   - make fuzz-smoke RUST_FUZZ_BINARY=path/to/rust_target
   - Passes --rust-binary flag to fuzz_differential (from bd-rkql.4)

2. Create tools/ci/gate_fuzz.sh wrapping the Makefile targets:
   - Accepts --tier smoke|nightly|release
   - Checks if Rust binary available, skips differential if not
   - Always runs C-only determinism checks (existing behavior)
   - Adds Rust comparison when binary available
   - JSONL logging per harness and aggregate

3. Add GATE-FUZZ to master gate suite (make check-ci already runs fuzz-smoke)

## Acceptance Criteria
- make fuzz-smoke continues to work without Rust binary (existing behavior preserved)
- make fuzz-smoke RUST_FUZZ_BINARY=... adds differential comparison
- gate_fuzz.sh has JSONL output, graceful SKIP when Rust unavailable
- Smoke tier < 60 seconds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:28:46.426638856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:16:06.661486280Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:16:06.658921561Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.7","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:28:46.426638856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.7","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:28:51.957899553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-rkql.8","title":"Standalone fuzz harness sanity tests with crash/UB detection logging","description":"## STATUS: INDEPENDENT TRACK — RUN EXISTING CODE UNDER SANITIZERS

## Context
fuzz_differential.c and fuzz_minimize.c already exist and are complete. This bead
runs them under ASan/UBSan to find memory safety issues or undefined behavior.

This bead is on an INDEPENDENT TRACK — it does not depend on the grammar audit
(rkql.1) or C harness audit (rkql.2). It uses the existing code as-is.

## Task
1. Build fuzz_differential with -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer
2. Run: fuzz_differential --nightly (100K iterations) under ASan+UBSan
3. Build fuzz_minimize with same sanitizer flags
4. Run: fuzz_minimize --selftest under ASan+UBSan
5. Verify determinism under sanitizers: 10K iterations, same seed, run twice,
   compare all digests — must be bit-identical
6. Create tests/fuzz/test_harness_sanity.sh automating all of the above

## JSONL Log Format
{"ts":"...","step":"asan_fuzz_differential","iterations":100000,"status":"pass","findings":0,"duration_ms":...}
{"ts":"...","step":"asan_fuzz_minimize_selftest","status":"pass","findings":0,"duration_ms":...}
{"ts":"...","step":"determinism_check","iterations":10000,"seed":"fixed","status":"pass","digest_match":true}

## Acceptance Criteria
- 100K iterations under ASan+UBSan, zero findings
- All self-tests pass under sanitizers
- Determinism verified under sanitizers (same seed → same digests)
- test_harness_sanity.sh exists with JSONL output
- If sanitizer finds issues, file them as new beads with P0 priority

## Why Independent Track
Sanitizer runs validate the EXISTING code, not the audited/modified code. Running
them early catches lurking UB that might otherwise be masked. Any findings here
inform the audit work (rkql.2) rather than depending on it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:30:33.966622688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T20:11:42.267349564Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T20:11:42.266429382Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.8","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:30:33.966622688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-va8n.2","title":"Create .clang-tidy config and install clang-tidy locally","description":"## Context
clang-tidy provides static analysis beyond what the compiler warns about. The CI
pipeline runs it as the lint gate, but locally it reports SKIP.

**CRITICAL FINDING:** .clang-tidy DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. It is not
merely missing from the local toolchain — the configuration file itself has never
been created. This bead must CREATE it, not just verify it.

## Background
Without .clang-tidy, clang-tidy runs with default checks, which produces too many
false positives on C99 code. A well-tuned .clang-tidy file enables useful checks
while suppressing irrelevant ones.

## Task
1. **Create .clang-tidy** in the repository root with C99-appropriate checks:
   - Enable: bugprone-*, cert-*, clang-analyzer-*, readability-implicit-bool-conversion
   - Disable: modernize-* (C++ only), cppcoreguidelines-* (C++ only),
     readability-magic-numbers (too noisy for embedded constants),
     bugprone-easily-swappable-parameters (too noisy for callback-heavy API)
   - Configure WarningsAsErrors for critical checks (null-dereference, use-after-free)
   - Add HeaderFilterRegex to only check project headers (not system headers)
   - Document rationale for each enabled/disabled check group
2. Install clang-tidy (version >= 17) on the local build host
3. Run clang-tidy on all source files with the project configuration
4. Triage findings:
   - Fix real bugs immediately
   - Suppress false positives with NOLINT + explanation comment
   - Adjust check configuration to reduce noise without hiding real issues
5. Verify make lint passes locally (no longer reports SKIP)

## Check Selection Methodology
- Start with a conservative set (bugprone-*, cert-*)
- Run on full codebase, categorize all findings
- If a check produces >10 false positives and <2 real bugs, disable it
- Document the triage in the .clang-tidy file as comments

## Acceptance Criteria
- .clang-tidy exists in repository root and is committed
- clang-tidy --version reports >= 17
- make lint exits 0 (PASS, not SKIP)
- Zero un-triaged findings
- Each disabled check has a documented rationale
- NOLINT annotations include explanation (e.g., // NOLINT(bugprone-foo): reason)

## Testing
- Run: clang-tidy src/**/*.c -- -Iinclude/ -std=c99
- Run: make lint
- Verify zero warnings/errors on clean code
- Intentionally introduce a bug (null deref) and verify clang-tidy catches it
- Remove the intentional bug after verification

## Why This Matters
Static analysis catches bugs that tests miss (especially UB, null derefs, type
confusion). Creating .clang-tidy with tuned checks gives meaningful results
instead of a wall of false positives.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.301315974Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.299330107Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-va8n.2","depends_on_id":"bd-va8n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -146,7 +146,9 @@ ASX_API asx_adaptive_policy asx_adaptive_policy_active(void);
  *
  * Preconditions: surface, posterior, and out_decision must not be NULL.

@@ -168,8 +170,10 @@ ASX_API int asx_adaptive_ledger_overflowed(void);
 
 /* Read entry at logical index (0 = oldest readable).
  * Returns nonzero on success, 0 on out-of-bounds.
- * The returned entry's pointers (surface, evidence labels) are valid
- * only until the next asx_adaptive_decide() or asx_adaptive_reset(). */
+ * The returned entry borrows caller-owned pointers (surface name and
+ * evidence labels). They remain valid only as long as the caller's
+ * backing storage remains live, and the entry contents may be overwritten
+ * by a later asx_adaptive_decide() after the ring wraps. */
 ASX_API int asx_adaptive_ledger_get(uint32_t index, asx_adaptive_ledger_entry *out);

@@ -99,17 +99,19 @@ ASX_API uint32_t asx_timer_collect_expired(asx_timer_wheel *wheel, asx_time now,
 /* -------------------------------------------------------------------
  * Timer update (cancel + re-register)
  *
- * Cancels the old timer and registers a new one with a fresh handle.
- * Returns ASX_OK on success. If the old handle is stale, the new
- * timer is still registered (old cancel is a no-op).
+ * Registers the replacement timer before retiring the old one so the
+ * operation is failure-atomic. If the new registration fails, the old
+ * timer remains live. If the old handle is stale, the new timer is
+ * still registered (old cancel is a no-op).
  * ------------------------------------------------------------------- */
 
-/* Cancel old timer and register a new one with updated deadline.
+/* Register a new timer and then retire the old one with updated deadline.
  *
  * Preconditions: wheel and out_handle must not be NULL.
  * old_handle may be NULL (treated as a pure register operation).
  * Returns ASX_OK on success, ASX_E_INVALID_ARGUMENT if NULL params,
- *   ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED if timer arena is full.
+ *   ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED if timer arena is full, and leaves the old
+ *   timer untouched on registration failure.
  * Thread-safety: not thread-safe; single-threaded mode only. */
 ASX_API ASX_MUST_USE asx_status asx_timer_update(asx_timer_wheel *wheel,
